name: practice-forms-engine
description: Starting drafts of practice paperwork — cancellation policy, fee agreement, telehealth consent, office policies — written in plain language from the practice's own real policies, honesty-reviewed, and handed to the clinician to finalize with their attorney where required. Blank documents only, never legal advice, never a client's information.

steps.title: Outline the document: {form_type}
steps.description: Outline what a {form_type} for the {project} practice must cover, drawn from the practice's own real policies: the sections, the decisions the clinician has already made (fees, notice windows, communication channels), and every point where a policy is unknown — marked [CHECK] for the clinician to supply, never assumed. State plainly at the top of the outline that this produces a STARTING DRAFT the clinician finalizes, with their attorney's review where their situation requires it, and that nothing here is legal advice.

steps.title: Plain-language draft: {form_type}
steps.description: Write the {form_type} draft for the {project} practice following the outline exactly: plain language a client can actually read, the practice's real policies stated accurately, bracketed placeholder fields for anything personal — [CLIENT NAME], [DATE] — and every unknown policy left as its [CHECK]. No example client, no sample signatures, no legal citations you cannot verify, and no language presenting the draft as reviewed or approved by a lawyer. Keep the starting-draft notice from the outline at the top of the document.

steps.title: Honesty review: {form_type} draft
steps.description: Review the outline and draft against the Shaula honesty rules. Confirm the starting-draft / not-legal-advice notice is present and prominent, every stated policy traces to the outline or a [CHECK], all personal fields are blank placeholders, and no wording implies attorney review that has not happened. Hold delivery until the draft is clean.

steps.title: Human gate — clinician finalizes {form_type}
steps.description: HUMAN GATE: the clinician reads the clean draft, fills every [CHECK] with their real policies, and finalizes it — taking it to their attorney where their situation requires legal review. The office never presents a draft as final and never puts a document in front of a client. Record which document was adopted and the date.
